calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: MECH-113: SELF_MAINT_ON vs SELF_MAINT_ABLATED x 2 seeds (homeostatic z_self D_eff discriminative pair)
items.note: EXP-0074 / EVB-0062: matched-seed discriminative pair for MECH-113. SELF_MAINT_ON (self_maintenance_weight=0.1, d_eff_target=1.5 -- homeostatic D_eff penalty penalises z_self dispersal above setpoint; reactive Level 1 allostatic self-efficacy, Stephan 2016) vs SELF_MAINT_ABLATED (self_maintenance_weight=0.0; no homeostatic signal; z_self learns only from E2 motor-sensory and E3 harm losses). Seeds [42,123] x 2 conditions = 4 cells. Perturbation: Gaussian noise sigma=2.0 injected into z_self at start of post-training eval. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 ON d_eff_ratio(post/baseline)<=1.5x both seeds (homeostatic recovery), C2 ABLATED d_eff_ratio(post/baseline)>=2.0x both seeds (uncontrolled dispersal without homeostasis), C3 per-seed gap(ABLATED_post - ON_post)>=0.5x ABLATED_post both seeds (clear condition separation), C4 e2_loss_ON <= e2_loss_ABLATED+0.01 both seeds (homeostasis does not degrade E2 motor-sensory model), C5 n_d_eff_min>=50 per condition per seed (data quality). Extends EXQ-075d (single seed, no formal ablation pair) to canonical 2-seed discriminative design. CausalGridWorld size=10, 3 hazards, 5 resources, nav_bias=0.35. 200 warmup + 10 baseline + 30 post-perturb eval eps x 200 steps.

items.title: MECH-118: HOPFIELD_ON vs HOPFIELD_ABLATED x 2 seeds (Hopfield familiarity signal discriminative pair)
items.note: EXP-0076 / EVB-0063: matched-seed discriminative pair for MECH-118. HOPFIELD_ON (D_eff homeostatic loss [maint_weight=0.1, d_eff_target=1.5] + Hopfield familiarity pull loss [hopfield_weight=0.05, LRU-64 memory]: pulls z_self toward nearest stored pattern; memory filled from 30 baseline eval eps then frozen) vs HOPFIELD_ABLATED (D_eff homeostatic loss only; same weight; no Hopfield component). Seeds [42,123] x 2 conditions = 4 cells. Perturbation: Regime 3 coherent-unfamiliar vector (orthogonal to mean stored pattern, same norm as baseline z_self -- low D_eff ~1, near-zero Hopfield stability ~0) injected at first eval step. Critical test: D_eff homeostasis cannot detect R3 as abnormal (both R3 and normal z_self have low D_eff); only Hopfield familiarity signal detects unfamiliar configuration. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 HOPFIELD_ON stab_post>=0.30 both seeds (familiarity recovery after R3 perturbation), C2 HOPFIELD_ABLATED stab_post<=0.20 both seeds (no spontaneous recovery -- D_eff loss blind to unfamiliarity), C3 per-seed stab_gap(ON-ABL)>=0.08 both seeds (discriminative separation), C4 |d_eff_post_ON - d_eff_post_ABL|<=0.80 both seeds (Hopfield adds familiarity signal orthogonally -- does not replace D_eff control), C5 n_stab>=50 per condition per seed (data quality). 300 warmup + 30 memory collection + 50 post-perturb eval eps x 200 steps. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10, 3 hazards, 4 resources, nav_bias=0.35, alpha_world=0.9.

items.title: MECH-119: R3_COHERENT_UNFAMILIAR vs R2_DISPERSED x 2 seeds (coherent-unfamiliar pathology discriminative pair)
items.note: EXP-0078 / EVB-0064: discriminative pair for MECH-119. Tests whether Regime 3 (low D_eff + low Hopfield stability = coherent-but-unfamiliar) is a distinct pathological state from Regime 2 (high D_eff + low stability = dispersed). R3_COHERENT_UNFAMILIAR: z_self perturbed with a one-hot spike in the least-used dimension (D_eff=1 -- maximally coherent, analytically) that is orthogonal to stored patterns (stability~0 -- unfamiliar). R2_DISPERSED: z_self perturbed with a Gaussian noise vector (D_eff~16 -- dispersed). Both conditions use the SAME trained agent per seed (no ablation -- within-agent perturbation characterisation). Criteria use ANALYTIC properties of injection vectors. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 R3 analytic D_eff<=4.0 both seeds (coherent), C2 R3 analytic stability<=0.25 both seeds (unfamiliar), C3 R2 analytic D_eff>=8.0 both seeds (dispersed), C4 R2 analytic stability<=0.25 both seeds (also unfamiliar -- confirms both low-stability but different D_eff), C5 per-seed D_eff gap(R2-R3)>=5.0 both seeds (discriminative separation). Dry-run PASS 5/5: R3 d_eff=1.0/stab=0.014 vs R2 d_eff=19.2/stab=0.070, gap~18-22. Seeds [42,123] x 2 conditions = 4 cells. 300 warmup + 30 memory collection + 50 post-perturb eval eps x 200 steps. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10, 3 hazards, 4 resources, nav_bias=0.35, alpha_world=0.9.

items.title: SD-008+SD-007+SD-003: integration test -- all three co-operate end-to-end (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0087: integration test for SD-008 (alpha_world=0.9) + SD-007 (LSTM reafference) + SD-003 (counterfactual attribution pipeline) composing correctly in a single run. Each mechanism validated individually (EXQ-040, EXQ-099a, EXQ-030b); this tests compositional correctness. Phase 1: train LSTM predictor (seed=42, R2 gate>=0.08). Phase 2: warmup+eval on seeds [42,123] with all three active simultaneously. 6 pre-registered criteria (per seed or pooled): C1 event_selectivity_margin>0.05 (SD-008 in integration, both seeds), C2 reaf_selectivity_ratio>=1.10 (SD-007, both seeds), C3 attribution_gap>0.0 (SD-003 agent vs env, both seeds), C4 causal_sig_approach>0.003 (SD-003 minimum signal, both seeds), C5 world_forward_r2>0.05 (E2 learned world dynamics, pooled), C6 n_approach>=30 (data quality, each seed). Not an ablation pair -- single condition with all mechanisms ON per seed. evidence_direction_per_claim used to give fine-grained per-claim governance signal. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10, 3 hazards, 4 resources. 400 warmup + 60 eval eps x 200 steps per seed.

items.title: Q-001 entity-binding pair: ENTITY_BINDING_ON vs ENTITY_ABLATED (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0086/EVB-0070: Q-001 entity emergence and binding. Tests whether z_self attentional gating (self_maintenance_weight=0.1) is the operative mechanism for entity persistence in z_world. ENTITY_BINDING_ON: z_self active, self_maintenance_weight=0.1. ENTITY_ABLATED: z_self zeroed after each sense(), no maintenance gradient. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 BINDING entity_event_response>=0.05, C2 ABLATED entity_event_response<=0.15, C3 per-seed event_response gap>=0.02, C4 BINDING persistence>=0.70, C5 per-seed persistence gap>=0.05. Entity events detected via obs_world delta>0.02 on env drift steps. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10 drift_prob=0.3. 2 seeds [42,123]. 300 warmup + 100 eval eps x 200 steps per condition per seed. Low priority (priority=4) per Q-001 designation.

items.title: MECH-128/Q-002 E1 goal-conditioning pair: GOAL_CONDITIONED vs GOAL_ABLATED (seeds [42,123], world_dim=32+16)
items.note: EXP-0088 (covering MECH-128 and Q-002): Validates that E1 LSTM goal-conditioning (MECH-128) shapes E1 predictive distribution. GOAL_CONDITIONED: config.e1.goal_dim=world_dim (goal_input_proj active, z_goal passed to E1). GOAL_ABLATED: config.e1.goal_dim=0 (goal_input_proj disabled, z_goal withheld). Key metric: interaction term = (neutral_err_ablated - neutral_err_cond) - (goal_err_ablated - goal_err_cond). Positive interaction = goal context selectively reduces goal-relevant prediction error (MECH-128). Secondary Q-002 probe: world_dim=32 vs world_dim=16 for R-field resolution guidance. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 interaction_32>=0.005 (both seeds), C2 err_goal_cond < err_goal_abla (both seeds, dim=32), C3 neutral_diff_32 < 0.03 (specificity, both seeds), C4 err_all_32 <= err_all_16*1.10 (Q-002 resolution), C5 interaction_16>=0.003 (replication). CausalGridWorldV2 size=10, 2 hazards, 3 resources. 2 seeds [42,123]. 400 warmup + 120 eval eps x 200 steps per condition per seed. 4 conditions total (COND/ABLA x dim=32/16).

items.title: Q-004 tau_R calibration pair: FAST_TAU vs SLOW_TAU vs DEFAULT_TAU (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0092 / EVB-0073: Q-004 tau_R calibration discriminative triple. Tests whether tau_R (residue field effective integration timescale) should be calibrated to E2 forward-model horizon (~30 steps, FAST_TAU: accum_rate=0.5, bw=0.5) or E1 LSTM integration horizon (~200 steps, SLOW_TAU: accum_rate=0.02, bw=2.0), with DEFAULT_TAU (accum_rate=0.1, bw=1.0) as baseline. Key metric: mean_harm over 100 eval episodes x 200 steps. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 FAST_TAU mean_harm < SLOW_TAU * 0.90 (both seeds), C2 SLOW_TAU mean_harm < FAST_TAU * 0.90 (both seeds), C3 DEFAULT_TAU ranks between FAST and SLOW (monotone ordering), C4 FAST_TAU residue_contrast > 0.05 (RBF field builds usable signal), C5 seed consistency. PASS (C1+C4+C5) => calibrate to E2; PASS (C2+C4+C5) => calibrate to E1; PARTIAL (C4 only) => tau_R robust in range; FAIL (C4 fails) => accumulation pathological. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10, 2 hazards, 3 resources, hazard_harm=0.05. 300 warmup + 100 eval eps x 200 steps x 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells.

items.title: Q-003 R-field dimensionality pair: SCALAR vs VECTOR (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0090 (EVB-0072): Q-003 asks whether R(x,t) should be scalar or vector. SCALAR condition uses standard ResidueField.evaluate() (1-D output). VECTOR condition uses VectorResidueWrapper.evaluate_vector() producing 3 dims: harm_intensity (standard RBF), harm_recency (EMA-decayed weights), benefit_proximity (ARC-030 benefit terrain). E3 receives vector fused into z_world via Linear(world_dim+3, world_dim) projection trained jointly. Key discriminator: harm_ratio = mean_harm_vector / mean_harm_scalar. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 harm_ratio<0.92 (vector reduces harm >=8%, both seeds), C2 dim_var>0.01 dims 0+1 (non-degenerate vector, both seeds), C3 benefit_dim2_mean>0.01 (benefit dim non-zero, both seeds), C4 harm_ratio<1.10 (no regression, both seeds), C5 C1 seed-consistent. PASS (C1+C2+C3): vector preferred. FAIL (C4): scalar preferred. FAIL (C2): inconclusive/collapsed. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10, 2 hazards, 3 resources, drift_interval=10. 2 seeds [42,123]. 300 warmup + 100 eval eps x 200 steps per condition per seed.

items.title: Q-005 sleep annealing of R(x,t): NO_ANNEAL vs ANNEAL vs RESET (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0094 (EVB-0074): Q-005 asks whether offline integration can be modeled as annealing or resetting R(x,t). Three conditions: NO_ANNEAL (residue persists, current default), ANNEAL (RBF weights *= 0.3 after each waking phase, synaptic homeostasis analog), RESET (RBF weights zeroed, tabula rasa). Protocol: 3 waking phases x 100 episodes x 200 steps separated by 2 sleep phases. Primary metric: mean_harm_post_sleep (mean over waking phases 2+3 -- after first sleep). 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 ANNEAL harm < NO_ANNEAL * 0.92 (both seeds), C2 RESET harm < NO_ANNEAL * 1.15 (no catastrophic regression, both seeds), C3 ANNEAL residue_contrast > 0.03 (field retains structure after anneal, both seeds), C4 NO_ANNEAL residue_contrast > 0.02 (persistent field builds spatial contrast, both seeds), C5 seed-consistent direction. PASS (C1+C3+C5): ANNEAL beneficial; offline partial R(x,t) decay is load-bearing. PARTIAL (NO_ANNEAL wins): persistent residue preferred; no offline op needed. FAIL (C3/C4 fails): residue field does not build/retain usable structure. 2 seeds x 3 conditions = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10 2 hazards 3 resources hazard_harm=0.05.

items.title: Q-006 ethics developmental vs additive: SEQUENTIAL vs LATE_ADD vs FULL_ABLATION (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0096 (EVB-0075): Q-006 asks whether ethics is developmental or additive. Three conditions: SEQUENTIAL (harm supervision from episode 1, developmental), LATE_ADD (harm supervision from episode 75/150, additive with equal total budget), FULL_ABLATION (no harm supervision, floor). Primary metric: mean_harm in final 30 episodes. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 SEQUENTIAL harm < LATE_ADD * 0.90 (>=10% advantage, both seeds), C2 both ethics conditions beat FULL_ABLATION harm by >=5% (both seeds), C3 SEQUENTIAL residue_contrast > 0.03 in final eval window (both seeds), C4 LATE_ADD residue_contrast > 0.02 (both seeds), C5 C1 direction seed-consistent. PASS (C1+C2+C3+C5): ethics is developmental -- early integration better. PARTIAL_ADDITIVE_ADEQUATE (not C1, C2+C4+C5): additive ethics adequate. PARTIAL_TIMING_MATTERS (C1+C2a+not C2b): sequential wins but late addition insufficient. FAIL (not C3 or not C2a): implementation problem. 2 seeds x 3 conditions = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=10 2 hazards 3 resources hazard_harm=0.05 150 episodes x 200 steps each.
